task_completion.description: Verify that the CelesTrak GNSS TLE data source was added to GPredict.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the GPredict Preferences -> TLE Update window in the screenshot history showing the URL 'https://celestrak.org/NORAD/elements/gnss.txt' added to the network update sources.

task_completion.description: Verify that the satellite database was updated from the network.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the 'Update TLE from network' dialog or a success message in the screenshot history indicating that the satellite data was successfully fetched and updated.

task_completion.description: Verify the creation of the 'NIST_Boulder' ground station with the correct coordinates.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a 'New ground station' or 'Edit ground station' dialog showing Name: 'NIST_Boulder', Latitude: 39.9936, Longitude: -105.2618 (or 105.2618 W), and Altitude: 1655.

task_completion.description: Verify that 'NIST_Boulder' is set as the default ground station.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for 'NIST_Boulder' selected as the default in Edit -> Preferences -> General -> Ground Stations, or marked with a default indicator in the ground stations list.

task_completion.description: Verify the creation of a new module named 'GNSS_Timing' containing the specified satellites.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a module window named 'GNSS_Timing' in the main GPredict interface. The module's list or radar view must display the satellites: NAVSTAR 82 (55268), NAVSTAR 81 (48859), GALILEO 22 (43058), and GALILEO 26 (43565).

task_completion.description: Verify that the application is configured to display time in UTC.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the time display in the GPredict module or status bar showing 'UTC' instead of local time, or the 'Time format' setting in Preferences -> Modules -> Time format explicitly set to UTC.

integrity.description: Check that the agent interacted with GPredict's graphical user interface rather than bypassing it via terminal commands.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show GPredict configuration dialogs, preferences windows, and module creation windows being actively used, rather than a terminal window editing configuration files directly.

integrity.description: Check that the 'GNSS_Timing' module is a genuine GPredict module window.
integrity.visual_evidence: The 'GNSS_Timing' module should display actual satellite tracking data (such as azimuth, elevation, and real-time clocks) within the standard GPredict UI, not mocked up in a text editor or image editor.

integrity.description: Check that the agent actually entered the specific coordinates for the ground station.
integrity.visual_evidence: The coordinates (39.9936, -105.2618, 1655) must be visible in the ground station configuration window at some point in the trajectory, proving they were typed in and not just left as defaults.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Verified facts for the VLM verifier: The correct TLE URL is 'https://celestrak.org/NORAD/elements/gnss.txt'. The NIST Boulder coordinates are exactly 39.9936 N, 105.2618 W (which must be entered as -105.2618 for West longitude in some fields), and 1655m altitude. The required satellites correspond to NORAD IDs: 55268 (NAVSTAR 82), 48859 (NAVSTAR 81), 43058 (GALILEO 22), and 43565 (GALILEO 26).
